meaning: adj.精巧的,技巧熟练的adv.技巧精湛地
examples: The script was workmanlike at best.
Really it's a workmanlike conference rather than a dramatic one.
The team produced a very workmanlike performance.
This is a tablet that feels sturdy without feeling heavy, and useful without feeling workmanlike.
Shrewbury were no more than workmanlike, and home keeper Roger Freestone was redundant in the opening half.
